zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert le Bigod於大約1015年出生在法國諾門迪阿福朗徹斯時，他的父親Toustien LeGoz26歲，母親Judith DeMontanolier83歲。他和Helene de Saint Sauveur至少有2個女兒。他於1066年登記服兵役。他去世於大約1071年在法國福朗-可特艾尤拉昌享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Canut the Great
historicEvents.description: As Aethelred, King of England died, his son, Edmund Ironside devised a truce with Danish King Canute. The truce divided the kingdom between the two but Edmund died shortly after and became King of England. Several years later, he also became king of Denmark.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert le Bigod was born about 1015, in Avranches, Normandy, France, his father, Toustien LeGoz, was 26 and his mother, Judith DeMontanolier, was 83. He had at least 2 daughters with Helene de Saint Sauveur. He registered for military service in 1066. He died about 1071, in Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert le Bigod est né environ 1015, à Avranches, Normandie, France, son père, Toustien LeGoz, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Judith DeMontanolier, avait 83 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Helene de Saint Sauveur. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1066. Il est décédé environ 1071, à Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert le Bigod um 1015 in Avranches, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Toustien LeGoz 26 und seine Mutter Judith DeMontanolier 83. Er hatte mit Helene de Saint Sauveur mindestens 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1066 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb um 1071 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert le Bigod è nato il circa 1015 nel Avranches, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Toustien LeGoz, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Judith DeMontanolier, aveva 83 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Helene de Saint Sauveur. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1066. Morì circa 1071, nel Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert le Bigodが約1015年にフランスノーマンディアvランチェスで生まれた時，父Toustien LeGozは26歳，母Judith DeMontanolierは83歳であった。彼はHelene de Saint Sauveurとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1066年に兵役登録をした。彼は約1071年にフランスフランチェ-コムトeジュラチャノンで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert le Bigod(이)가 대략 1015년 프랑스 노먼디 아브랭체스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Toustien LeGoz(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith DeMontanolier(은)는 83세였다. 그는 Helene de Saint Sauveur(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1066년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 대략 1071년 프랑스 프랭체-코트e 라 챈넌에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1015–about 1071: tany ho any amin'ny 1015–tany ho any amin'ny 1071

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert le Bigod nasceu aproximadamente 1015, em Avranches, Normandie, França, seu pai, Toustien LeGoz, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Judith DeMontanolier, tinha 83 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Helene de Saint Sauveur. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1066. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1071, em Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, França, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert le Bigod родился около 1015 г. в местности авранчесь, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Toustien LeGoz было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith DeMontanolier, 83. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Helene de Saint Sauveur. В 1066 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер около 1071 г. в местности чанон, джура, фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 58 лет.

sm.about 1015–about 1071: e tusa o le 1015–e tusa o le 1071

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert le Bigod nació aproximadamente 1015, en Avranches, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Toustien LeGoz, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Judith DeMontanolier, tenía 83 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Helene de Saint Sauveur. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1066. Murió aproximadamente 1071, en Chanon, Jura, Franche-Comté, Francia, a la edad de 58 años.
